mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real retail sales indicate a broad-based demand contraction across residential and consumer sectors, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 50%) over 60-day horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 55% when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns exceed 55% when CPIAUCSL > 200, or Rank IC < 0.03 in high-inflation subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive returns (accuracy < 50%) over 20-day horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals strong aggregate demand and economic expansion, which immediately lifts equity market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts, where manufacturing expands while residential construction contracts, signals structural economic imbalances that eventually trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 50%) over 60-day horizon in walk-forward tests.
